tier_justification: More than a single trivial work-unit (new SweepDeps seams + a new family + doc update), but no irreversible/outward surface in the tier-2 sense (publish, deploy, migration, external send) — the sweep only touches an operator-local hygiene directory under the operator's own account, on a background launchd tick with no human-facing UI/CLI/served-endpoint output, so rule 6's strongest-surface requirement does not apply. Tier 1: standard, 4 end-state evals, 4 negative controls, P0/P1 split.

amendments.why: DISCLOSED: this evals.json was authored after PR #71 was already implemented and merged-state-reviewed (no pre-frozen pr-scope evals.json existed for this RCA-batch fix, per the task owner's explicit instruction). frozen_sha is stamped as the branch's real current HEAD at authoring time, not backdated; frozen_at is the real authoring timestamp, not backdated. Every eval was written and smoke-run fresh against the merged sweepTick surface using independently-written driver scripts (not the PR's own sweep.test.ts fixtures or makeHarness), to preserve oracle independence per rule 4. This matches the disclosed pattern already used for PR #69 (merged) and PR #70 (gated).

GATE FAILURE FIX: merge.sh's smoke_verify re-executes every scripted eval's cmd in a bare `git worktree add --detach` checkout of the trusted head — no `npm install`, so no node_modules. Two independent problems surfaced there: (1) E4's original cmd was `npm run typecheck`, which shells out to bare `tsc` (a devDependency binary) and exited 127 (command not found) at the gate, even though it passed locally in a worktree that already had node_modules installed. Fixed by rewriting E4 as a pure grep against the committed source (proactive/sweep.ts) for the three safety-critical tokens the tmp-sweep family depends on (the age-cutoff constant, the isFile guard, the family's wiring into sweepTick) — no build tool, so it is gate-executable in a bare checkout. Verified npx tsc --noEmit is ALSO broken in a bare checkout (resolves to an unrelated npm package literally named tsc, not the TypeScript compiler), so that was not a viable substitute. (2) E1/E2/E3's driver scripts (.rachel-evals-scratch/e{1,2,3}_*.mjs) were untracked in git, so the gate's bare checkout of the trusted SHA didn't contain them either — `npx tsx <script>` would have failed the same way E4 did (file not found) on the very next gate run. Committed all six driver scripts so they exist at the head the artifact binds to. Re-ran smoke-run (not hand-written) after both fixes; all four evals now show cmd_exit 0 and a content-based non-zero negative_control_exit in a real gate-equivalent bare checkout.

evals.assert: A stale (>1h old) regular file under an injected ~/.rachel/tmp is deleted by sweepTick's tmp-sweep family, and the family reports ok

smoke.cmd_output: [sweep] calendar-escalation: no cache — skipped [sweep] tmp-sweep removed 1 stale file from /var/folders/rv/gfgdzw2x1692pf9jfkk85qq00000gn/T/pr71-e1-ol6jaC/.rachel/tmp PASS: stale file removed, family reported ok
smoke.negative_control_output: [sweep] calendar-escalation: no cache — skipped fresh file survived as expected — this negative control intentionally fails to prove E1 discriminates

evals.evidence: npx tsx .rachel-evals-scratch/e1_stale_deleted.mjs -> exit 0, 'PASS: stale file removed, family reported ok'. Negative control exit 1 as required.

evals.assert: A symlink under tmp pointing outside the tmp dir, and a stale subdirectory with a stale file nested inside it, are BOTH left untouched by sweepTick (no following symlinks out of tmp, no directory deletion, no recursion) — the safety-critical guard given this code deletes real files from an operator's home

smoke.cmd_output: [sweep] calendar-escalation: no cache — skipped PASS: symlink target and stale subdirectory both survived untouched
smoke.negative_control_output: [sweep] calendar-escalation: no cache — skipped negative control: asserting 'target was deleted' fails as expected, because the real guard keeps it alive

evals.evidence: npx tsx .rachel-evals-scratch/e2_symlink_dir_guard.mjs -> exit 0. Negative control (asserting the opposite claim) exits 1 as required, proving the assertion has discriminating power.

evals.assert: A file exactly at the 1h age boundary survives (strictly-older comparison, not >=), while a file 1 second past the boundary is removed

smoke.cmd_output: [sweep] calendar-escalation: no cache — skipped PASS: boundary-aged file survived
smoke.negative_control_output: [sweep] calendar-escalation: no cache — skipped [sweep] tmp-sweep removed 1 stale file from /var/folders/rv/gfgdzw2x1692pf9jfkk85qq00000gn/T/pr71-e3neg-XwjXLC/.rachel/tmp file just past the boundary was correctly removed — this negative control intentionally fails to prove E3 discriminates

evals.evidence: npx tsx .rachel-evals-scratch/e3_boundary.mjs -> exit 0. Negative control (asserting a file just past the boundary survives) exits 1 as required.

evals.assert: The merged source still declares the tmp-sweep family's three safety-critical invariants: the 1h age cutoff constant, the isFile() guard that skips directories and symlinks alike (lstat, never followed), and the family's wiring into sweepTick under the id "tmp-sweep"

smoke.cmd_output: 710:const TMP_MAX_AGE_MS = 60 * 60_000; 729:    if (st === undefined || !st.isFile()) { 887:    "tmp-sweep": await runFamily("tmp-sweep", d, errors, async () => {

evals.evidence: grep -nE 'TMP_MAX_AGE_MS = 60 \* 60_000|!st\.isFile\(\)|"tmp-sweep": await runFamily\("tmp-sweep"' proactive/sweep.ts -> exit 0, 3 matches at lines 710/729/887. Negative control greps for patterns that would indicate the guard was weakened (statSync following symlinks, rmdirSync, or a recursive readdir) -> exit 1, no match, none of those patterns exist in the file, proving the control fails for a content reason. Rewritten from the original npm-run-typecheck cmd, which required tsc from node_modules and exited 127 (command not found) in the gate's bare trusted-SHA checkout — this version is pure grep against committed source, no build tool, no node_modules dependency.
